LAW AND MOTION, 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT MS. JUNG IS A FORMER LEGAL RESEARCH ATTORNEY IN DEPT. 206 AND SHE WORKED WITH HER AT THAT TIME. THE COURT BELIEVES THAT THIS IS NOT A BASIS FOR HER RECUSAL. COUNSEL WAS GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTION. NO QUESTION WAS ASKED OR OBJECTION WAS RAISED. DEFENDANTS JEE LAW AND JEE LAW, PLLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF ESTABLISHES JURISDICTION UNDER BROWN V. WATSON (1989) 207 CAL.APP.3D 1306. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE EXERCISE OF JURISDICTION WOULD BE UNREASONABLE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash, And In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
ANDREW MARRINER's Motion To Quash, And In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order - The motion is transferred to be heard in Department 301 (Discovery calendar) at 9:00 a.m. on the same date. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN P. FLYNN OF THE MCLEOD, MOSCARINO & FLYNN LLP, TEL. NO. 874-3405 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CLARA JUNG OF THE KAUFHOLD GASKIN LLP, TEL. NO. 944-9408 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT MS. JUNG IS A FORMER LEGAL RESEARCH ATTORNEY IN DEPT. 206 AND SHE WORKED WITH HER AT THAT TIME. THE COURT BELIEVES THAT THIS IS NOT A BASIS FOR HER RECUSAL. COUNSEL WAS GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTION. NO QUESTION WAS ASKED OR OBJECTION WAS RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JEE LAW and JEE LAW, PLLC's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied. Plaintiff establishes jurisdiction under Brown v. Watson (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 1306. Defendant fails to show that the exercise of jurisdiction would be unreasonable. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Other Party Andrew Marriner'S Motion To Quash, And In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on November 4, 2013:
John P. Flynn, Esq. of McLeod, Moscarino, Witham & Flynn LLP [415.874.3410] appearing for Plaintiff MICHAEL V. PETRAS;
Diane M. Barker, Esq. of Trenk DiPasquale [510.891.8687] appearing for Non-Party/Other Party ANDREW MARRINER

HEARING - R U L I N G: Other Party ANDREW MARRINER'S Motion To Quash, And In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order is taken under submission. Andrew Marriner's motion to quash and in the alternative for protective order. Heard and taken under submission on November 4, 2014, court order transferring Andrew Marriner's motion to Department 304 for further consideration. Judge Pro Tem: Robert F. Kane.
